title: A Giant Hole on a Metal Asteroid
summary: Scientists are studying a big asteroid called Psyche in space between Mars and Jupiter. It is made mostly of metal and is very large. NASA is sending a spacecraft to visit it in 2029 to learn more. Researchers think Psyche might be the leftover heart of an old planet that broke apart, or it could have formed as a metal object from the start. To find out, they use computer simulations to look at a huge crater on Psyche's surface. This crater can show what is inside the asteroid, like if it has layers or is mixed up. Understanding Psyche helps us learn how planets like Earth were made long ago.

keywords.explanation: a rocky or metal object that orbits the sun
a shiny material like iron or steel
a big hole made by something hitting the surface
computer programs that pretend to show how things happen
a machine that travels in space to explore
a large round object like Earth that goes around the sun

questions.correct_answer: It shows what is inside the asteroid

questions.question: What might Psyche be the leftover heart of?

background_read: Asteroids are small objects in space that can tell us about the early solar system. Psyche is special because it is big and made of metal, unlike most asteroids that are rocky. Scientists want to know if it was once part of a planet that broke apart. They use tools like simulations to guess what is inside before a spacecraft goes there to check. Learning about Psyche helps us understand how planets like Earth formed billions of years ago.
Article_Structure: WHAT: Studying a metal asteroid called Psyche and its big crater
WHERE: In the asteroid belt between Mars and Jupiter
WHY: To learn if it is the heart of a lost planet and how planets form

perspectives.description: Using technology to explore space and discover secrets about asteroids.
